rationale: The cited paper is used once in the conceptual background as an illustrative example that an exciting brand personality can increase word of mouth via arousal. In the citing paper, this serves as general support for the broader claim that excitement affects consumer behavior, alongside another example citation. The core theory, hypotheses, measures, and empirical tests in the citing study focus on logo asymmetry, congruence, and brand equity rather than WOM, so this citation functions as background rather than a direct building block.
